prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
In the reign of Fiscal Lord Q3, I was summoned from my cloister to chronicle the deeds of a kingdom built not of stone but of clouds, wherein servers hum like bees entombed in a silver reliquary and tributes recur each moon by some inscrutable pact called SaaS. 
The annals show that the Great Revenue, Annual and Monthly, did wax like a well-fed dragon—net retention surpassing a full hundred by several brave points, while the heresy of Churn was put to flight and the Upsell brethren laid many new coins upon the altar. 
The keepers of the Ledger aver that the cost of wooing a fresh soul (they name it C-A-C, which I take for “Capture A Customer”) was but a modest dowry compared to the life-tithe known as L-T-V, and though candles burned swiftly in the treasury, the Runway-street yet stretches past two harvests if the wind be at our back. 
Each morn the guild holds a Stand-Up like hurried matins before the idol Zoom, and having smitten many demons from the code with sprinting pilgrimages, they present unto me charts bright as illuminated constellations, from which I conclude Q3 was prosperous indeed, though by my troth I still cannot fathom how their cloud rains money and not water.
